efficacyPairs.questionText: Two angles on a straight line measure 115° and x°. What is the value of x?
lessonLearningGoal: How to find a missing angle on a straight line
Why angles on a straight line always add up to 180°

efficacyPairs.questionText: Emma draws three angles around a point. Two angles are 100° and 130°. What is the third angle?
lessonLearningGoal: How to find a missing angle around a point
Why angles around a point always add up to 360°

efficacyPairs.questionText: A triangle has angles of 70° and 55°. What is the third angle?
Jake measures an angle and finds it is 135°. What type of angle is this?
lessonLearningGoal: How to tell the difference between acute (less than 90°), right, obtuse (between 90° and 180°), straight, and reflex (more than 180°) angles
Why the size of an angle matters, not the length of the lines

efficacyPairs.questionText: An equilateral triangle has all three sides equal. What is each angle in an equilateral triangle?
Sophie is building a rectangular frame. What is the sum of all four angles in a rectangle?
lessonLearningGoal: How to find a missing angle in a quadrilateral (a four-sided shape)
Why the four angles in any quadrilateral add up to 360°

efficacyPairs.questionText: Two angles in a triangle are 45° and 90°. What type of triangle is this?
efficacyPairs.subConceptName: Missing Angle in a Right-Angled Triangle
lessonLearningGoal: How to use the right angle (90°) as a known angle
Why the other two angles in a right-angled triangle add up to 90°

efficacyPairs.questionText: Tom draws an isosceles triangle with two equal angles of 65° each. What is the third angle?
lessonLearningGoal: How to spot an isosceles triangle (a triangle with two equal sides) from the tick marks
Why two equal sides means two equal angles

efficacyPairs.questionText: Four angles meet at a point. Three of them are 85°, 90°, and 105°. What is the fourth angle?
lessonLearningGoal: How to find a missing angle around a point
Why angles around a point always add up to 360°

efficacyPairs.questionText: Lucy draws a quadrilateral with angles 80°, 100°, and 95°. What is the fourth angle?
lessonLearningGoal: How to find a missing angle in a quadrilateral (a four-sided shape)
Why the four angles in any quadrilateral add up to 360°

efficacyPairs.questionText: An angle measures 220°. What type of angle is this?
lessonLearningGoal: How to tell the difference between acute (less than 90°), right, obtuse (between 90° and 180°), straight, and reflex (more than 180°) angles
Why the size of an angle matters, not the length of the lines

efficacyPairs.questionText: Ben measures angles on a straight line. One angle is 3x° and the other is 2x°. What is the value of x?
lessonLearningGoal: How to set up an equation when angles are given as expressions like 2x or 3x + 10
Why you must substitute back to find the actual angle, not just x

efficacyPairs.questionText: A triangle has two angles that are each x°, and a third angle of 80°. What is x?
lessonLearningGoal: How to set up an equation when angles are given as expressions like 2x or 3x + 10
Why you must substitute back to find the actual angle, not just x

efficacyPairs.questionText: Oliver draws two parallel lines crossed by a third line. An angle on one side is 65°. What is the corresponding angle on the other parallel line?
lessonLearningGoal: How to spot corresponding angles (in matching positions) and alternate angles (in Z-shapes) with parallel lines (lines that never meet)
Why these angles are equal when lines are parallel

lessonLearningGoal: How to see that the exterior angle (the angle outside the shape) equals the two opposite interior angles (inside angles)
